channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fans gather at FNB Stadium ahead of Orlando Pirates vs Kaizer Chiefs derby
item.description: Thousands of football supporters began streaming into Johannesburg’s FNB Stadium on Sunday as anticipation built for one of South Africa’s biggest sporting events, the Soweto Derby. The highly anticip...

item.title: Protester Arrested After Spraying Chemical Agent on LAPD Officers during Demonstration
item.description: A protester was arrested after spraying a chemical agent on two Los Angeles Police Department officers outside the Metropolitan Detention Center on April 25, 2026. The confrontation took place during ...

item.title: Anne Curtis reflects on ‘Endo’ experience and praises Jasmine Curtis-Smith’s performance
item.description: Nagbahagi si Anne Curtis ng kanyang mga natutunan mula sa panonood o karanasan kaugnay ng dulang “Endo,” habang ipinahayag din niya ang kanyang pagmamalaki sa kapatid na si Jasmine Curtis-Smith. Bagam...

item.title: WHO Warns Millions of African Children Still Missing Out on Life-Saving Vaccines
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) don yan say vaccines dey prevent around 1.8 million deaths every year for Africa, but wahala still dey as about 6.7 million children never even get one routine vacc...

item.title: Shots Fired Near White House Correspondents' Dinner; Trump Evacuated Safely
item.description: The White House Correspondents' Association dinner at the Washington Hilton turned chaotic on April 25, 2026, when a gunman armed with a shotgun, handgun, and knives attempted to breach a security che...

item.title: Kerala Grapples with Rising Snakebite Fatalities, Government Assistance Announced
item.description: Kerala is facing a concerning surge in snakebite incidents, with the death toll now reaching seven in recent days. The latest victims, two elderly women in Kannur and Idukki districts, succumbed to ve...

item.title: Van Dijk backs Isak to improve after injury-hit debut season at Liverpool
item.description: Virgil van Dijk has expressed confidence in Liverpool striker Alexander Isak despite the Swedish forward enduring a challenging first season at Anfield. Signed for a British record fee of £125 million...

item.title: Devotees mark Kanyaka Parameswari Jayanti with rituals and procession in Vizianagaram
item.description: Kanyaka Parameswari Jayanti was observed with great devotion and traditional enthusiasm in Vizianagaram on Sunday, drawing a large number of devotees to the temple dedicated to the goddess. The celebr...

item.title: Philippines Receives Information Regarding Zaldy Co’s Location in Czech Republic
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) in the Philippines has reportedly received a lead regarding the whereabouts of Zaldy Co, the subject of arrest warrants related to the Percy Lapid murder case. The info...

item.title: Zamfara APC Leader Injured During Highway Attack by Armed Bandits
item.description: For Zamfara State, wahala still dey for roads as armed bandits don attack political figure Abdullahi Marafa, wey be APC chieftain and former Commissioner for Religious Affairs. The attack happen on Sa...

item.title: Senior Maurice Blackburn lawyer Josh Bornstein to leave firm for new non-government role
item.description: Prominent Australian labour lawyer Josh Bornstein has announced he will step down from his role leading Maurice Blackburn’s employment practice, marking the end of a significant chapter in one of the ...

item.title: Exploring the Everyday Struggles and Quiet Resilience in Catherine Thankamma’s Short Stories
item.description: Catherine Thankamma’s collection 'A Kind of Meat and Other Stories' presents a series of narratives grounded in the ordinary yet significant tremors that influence daily life. The stories delve into p...

item.title: Kenya's Sawe breaks marathon world record with sub-two-hour run
item.description: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe made history on Sunday by becoming the first man ever to complete a marathon in under two hours. His remarkable performance at the London Marathon saw him finish with a time of ...

item.title: NTA Launches 24/7 Helpline and Advisory Ahead of NEET Exam
item.description: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has issued an important advisory for all students appearing for the upcoming NEET examinations, emphasising that no exam should take priority over one’s safety and we...

item.title: Explosive device detonates outside Dunmurry police station, targeting officers
item.description: An explosive device recently detonated outside a police station in Dunmurry, Northern Ireland, in what authorities have described as a targeted attack against law enforcement personnel. The incident c...

item.title: Shida Bazyar’s Novel Explores Iranian Revolution and Exile Across Generations
item.description: Shida Bazyar’s novel 'The Nights are Quiet in Tehran', translated from German by Ruth Martin, has been shortlisted for the 2026 International Booker Prize. The story spans three decades from 1979 to 2...

item.title: The curious case of Anders Fernstedt, the homeless man living outside London's largest house
item.description: The story of Anders Fernstedt, a homeless Swedish man, who has become the only resident of London’s largest private home, has captured the imagination of locals and visitors alike. Located at 2-8a Rut...

item.title: KZN Premier condemns brutal killing of seven Moonsamy family members and calls for community action
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal Premier Thamsanqa Ntuli has strongly condemned the brutal killing of seven members of the Moonsamy family, describing the tragedy as a devastating blow to the province. Speaking at a mas...

item.title: Funke Awodiya’s Celebration of Womanhood in Poetry
item.description: This article na review of Funke Awodiya’s poetry book titled 'Woman of a Woman', as Syl Cheney Coker take look am. The book get sixty pages full of poems wey dey celebrate woman for all her aspects. T...

item.title: Australian artist Peter Godwin wins $100k prize for Li River landscape despite initial doubts
item.description: Australian artist Peter Godwin has taken out a $100,000 art prize for a work inspired by the iconic karst mountain landscapes along China’s Li River, a subject long revered in traditional Chinese art....
